movies.title: Captain America: Brave New World
movies.overview: Sam Wilson, the new Captain America, finds himself in the middle of an international incident when he must uncover the motives behind a nefarious global plot.

movies.title: Paddington in Peru
movies.overview: Paddington returns to Peru to visit his beloved Aunt Lucy, who now resides at the Home for Retired Bears. With the Brown family in tow, a thrilling adventure ensues.

movies.title: Dog Man
movies.overview: A faithful police dog and his human cop companion are injured in the line of duty, and a life-saving surgery fuses them into one hybrid hero: Dog Man.

movies.title: Heart Eyes
movies.overview: A masked killer known as the Heart Eyes Killer targets couples in a small city on Valentine's Day, and two coworkers pretending to be a couple must survive the night.

movies.title: The Monkey
movies.overview: Twin brothers discover a cursed toy monkey that brings death to those around them. Decades later, the monkey resurfaces and the brothers must find a way to destroy it.

movies.title: Companion
movies.overview: A woman discovers that she is actually a lifelike AI companion during a weekend trip with friends, and must fight for her survival when things take a dark turn.

movies.title: One of Them Days
movies.overview: Two best friends and roommates must hustle to come up with rent money in one day after discovering one of their boyfriends has blown their funds.

movies.title: Elevation
movies.overview: In a post-apocalyptic world where monsters occupy altitudes below a certain elevation, a father must venture below the safe zone to save his son's life.

movies.title: You're Cordially Invited
movies.overview: Two families discover their weddings have been double-booked at the same venue, leading to an escalating battle of wills to claim the space.

movies.title: The Alto Knights
movies.overview: The true story of two of New York's most notorious organized crime bosses, Frank Costello and Vito Genovese, whose rivalry shaped the American Mafia.

predictions.reasoning: MCU brand still carries weight globally despite franchise fatigue. Anthony Mackie as new Cap has built-in audience from Disney+. Harrison Ford joining adds prestige draw. February release is unusual for MCU.
Paddington franchise has exceptional word-of-mouth and global appeal. Third installment benefits from brand loyalty. Antonio Banderas and Olivia Colman add star power.
Dav Pilkey's books have massive readership among kids. Captain Underpants movie did $125M worldwide. Dog Man books have outsold Captain Underpants. Should overperform.
Horror-thriller with Valentine's Day hook. Low budget genre film. Similar to Happy Death Day in concept. Should be solidly profitable but limited ceiling.
Stephen King brand is reliable for horror. Osgood Perkins gained massive buzz from Longlegs. Horror-comedy angle broadens appeal. Theo James is ascending.
AI-themed thriller taps into zeitgeist. Low budget sci-fi horror. Sophie Thatcher (Yellowjackets) has growing fanbase. Limited marketing push expected.
Buddy comedy with musical crossover appeal via SZA. Keke Palmer is a strong comedy lead. Primarily domestic play, limited international.
High-concept sci-fi action with Anthony Mackie. Modest budget suggests limited theatrical push. Genre has been hit-or-miss post-pandemic.
Star-driven comedy with Witherspoon and Ferrell. Wedding premise is broad and accessible. Both stars past their peak box office draw but still recognizable.
Robert De Niro in dual mob roles directed by Barry Levinson. Prestige crime drama with awards potential. Limited action appeal but strong older demographic draw.
MCU sequel with established IP, Harrison Ford addition. Post-Endgame MCU has been inconsistent, but Captain America brand is strong. Similar to Falcon & Winter Soldier audience.
Paddington franchise is beloved with excellent reviews. Third installment should perform similarly to Paddington 2. Strong international appeal.
Based on popular Dav Pilkey books (Captain Underpants creator). Strong built-in kid audience. Animation should do well internationally.
Valentine's Day horror is a niche but proven concept (Valentine, My Bloody Valentine). Low budget should make this profitable.
Stephen King adaptation with horror-comedy angle. Osgood Perkins has rising profile after Longlegs. Should perform similar to recent mid-budget horror.
AI thriller is timely but niche. Jack Quaid and Sophie Thatcher have rising profiles. Low budget means profitability is likely.
Comedy with Keke Palmer and SZA. SZA's massive music fanbase could drive opening. Limited international appeal though.
Anthony Mackie sci-fi action. Post-apocalyptic genre is well-worn. Mid-budget action with modest expectations.
Witherspoon + Ferrell is strong comedy pairing. Wedding comedies have proven track record. Should play well with mainstream audiences.
De Niro in a mob drama from Barry Levinson. Dual role performance could generate buzz but crime dramas have limited ceiling.
MCU continues to underperform relative to Infinity Saga. New Captain America lacks RDJ-level draw. Harrison Ford adds interest but audience skepticism about MCU Phase 5/6 is real.
Paddington is a reliable family franchise with excellent critical reception. Third films sometimes see diminishing returns. Strong UK/international markets.
Animated adaptation of popular children's book. Captain Underpants did modest business. New IP in animation is risky. Should find its audience with kids.
Valentine's Day horror release. Genre films with holiday hooks can overperform (Halloween, Christmas horror). Low budget ensures profitability.
Stephen King adaptation. Perkins coming off Longlegs success. Horror-comedy is a crowdpleaser genre. Moderate expectations.
Sci-fi thriller about AI. Timely concept. Low budget affair with rising cast. Ex Machina comparisons likely.
Comedy driven by Keke Palmer and SZA. Music-to-film crossover can drive opening weekends. Mostly domestic play.
Post-apocalyptic sci-fi action. Mackie has rising profile with Captain America. Modest genre entry.
Comedy stars Witherspoon and Ferrell. Wedding comedy premise is accessible. Streaming era has hurt theatrical comedy but these stars still draw.
De Niro mob drama. Prestige play from Levinson. The Irishman comparisons inevitable. Should find audience among older moviegoers.

MCU Captain America franchise has strong track record (Winter Soldier $715M, Civil War $1.15B). Anthony Mackie as new Cap is solid but less iconic than Evans; Harrison Ford as Ross/Red Hulk adds major star power and draw. Julius Onah unproven in blockbusters but Marvel IP carries. $250M budget typical. Feb 2026 release (like Ant-Man 3's $476M but this has higher profile/cast). Global plot appeals to fans. Post-Deadpool momentum boosts to ~$875M WW.

Paddington franchise averaged ~$250M WW previously with strong family appeal; consistent voice cast, added stars Banderas/Colman boost draw; $60M budget reasonable; January release tempers upside but IP ensures solid global performance around prior entries.

Based on Dav Pilkey's bestselling Dog Man book series (bigger than Captain Underpants, which grossed $125M WW in 2017), family animation comedy genre with broad kid appeal, modest $40M budget, solid comedic cast (Pete Davidson, Lil Rel Howery), January release for family counterprogramming, and director's animation TV experience. Adjusted for inflation and popularity, expects $250-350M WW range; $280M midpoint.

Low-budget ($12M) Valentine's Day slasher with holiday marketing hook and recognizable B-list cast (Mason Gooding from Scream, Jordana Brewster). Comparable to 'Thanksgiving' ($41M WW) but with stronger cast and timely Feb release; projects $50-70M WW driven by horror fanbase and VOD potential.

Low-budget ($18M) Stephen King horror-comedy adaptation directed by buzzworthy Osgood Perkins (Longlegs success). Solid cast with Theo James, Tatiana Maslany, Elijah Wood adds appeal. Feb 2026 release suits horror; expect 4-5x budget multiplier like recent hits (Longlegs ~$40M, Smile $217M), projecting $85M WW with strong US legs and moderate intl.

Low-budget sci-fi horror akin to M3GAN ($181M WW on $12M), with timely AI plot and solid rising cast (Thatcher, Quaid). January release limits upside vs. M3GAN's slot, new director caps star power, but genre legs and $15M budget support $95M WW.

Low-budget urban comedy with strong Black cast appeal (Keke Palmer, SZA crossover from music, Lil Rel Howery), fun roommate hustle plot. January MLK weekend release aids domestic legs ($30-35M est.), modest intl ($15-20M). Comps: The Blackening ($67M), lower due to smaller director/profile.

Low $20M budget sci-fi thriller with intriguing monster premise akin to 'A Quiet Place' rules; Anthony Mackie's MCU draw boosts appeal, but January dump release, modest director track record, and supporting cast limit it to mid-tier performance (~2.5-3x budget WW).

Nicholas Stoller's proven comedy track record (Neighbors $271M WW), star power of Reese Witherspoon and Will Ferrell in a fun wedding clash premise akin to Wedding Crashers/Ticket to Paradise ($172M WW), low $30M budget, and January release for counter-programming support $135M WW (est. $60M domestic + $75M intl).

Robert De Niro's star power in a dual gangster role draws fans, supported by Barry Levinson's direction, but February release, niche crime-drama genre, and modest $45M budget cap appeal to $60M+ WW amid competition and aging lead.
MCU fatigue is a factor. New Cap has less draw than Chris Evans. Harrison Ford joining is interesting. February superhero releases have mixed history.
Beloved franchise returns. Third films can see audience erosion. Strong family appeal and holiday legs. International markets crucial.
Popular children's book adaptation. Animation market is competitive. Dav Pilkey has proven audience. Similar trajectory to Captain Underpants but potentially bigger.
Low-budget horror with Valentine's Day release. Genre films in February can work. Limited star power but horror doesn't need it.
Stephen King IP + Perkins directing buzz. Horror-comedy blend appeals to wider audience than straight horror. Should perform well.
AI thriller with modest cast. Interesting concept but hard to market. Low budget keeps risk manageable.
Comedy with Keke Palmer and singer SZA. Music crossover could boost opening. Limited overseas appeal.
Sci-fi action with moderate budget. Post-apocalyptic genre is familiar territory. Mackie alone can't carry huge numbers.
Witherspoon and Ferrell comedy about double-booked weddings. Star power should drive opening. Comedy genre struggles theatrically but these are A-listers.
De Niro dual-role mob drama. Director Levinson has mixed recent track record. Crime genre has limited ceiling but De Niro name still carries weight.
MCU film with new Captain America. Superhero fatigue may limit performance. Harrison Ford adds draw. Should still clear $400M worldwide.
Third Paddington film. Family audience is reliable. May see some franchise fatigue. UK and international markets will be crucial.
Animated film based on children's books. New IP is always risky in animation. Book popularity should help. Modest expectations.
Horror film releasing on Valentine's Day. Niche audience. Low budget production. Should be profitable on small numbers.
Stephen King horror-comedy. Has built-in audience from King brand. Moderate budget and expectations.
Low budget AI thriller. Interesting concept. Limited marketing reach. Should find niche audience.
Comedy with Keke Palmer and SZA. Modest expectations. Primarily domestic release.
Post-apocalyptic sci-fi with Anthony Mackie. Modest budget and scale. Should find small audience.
Comedy with major stars. Wedding premise is familiar. Comedy genre has limited theatrical draw currently.
Robert De Niro mob film. Crime drama with limited audience. De Niro's star power has waned for theatrical releases.
